howItWorks.intro: This contraction timer calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: 4-1-1 Rule: contractions 4 min apart, lasting 1 min, for 1 hour = go to hospital
Early labor: contractions 5-20 min apart, 30-45 seconds
Active labor: contractions 3-5 min apart, 45-60+ seconds
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: When should I go to the hospital?
faq.answer: The 4-1-1 rule: when contractions are 4 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute each, for at least 1 hour. Also go immediately if: water breaks, heavy bleeding, baby stops moving, or you feel something is wrong.

faq.question: How do I time contractions?
faq.answer: Time from the START of one contraction to the START of the next (that's the frequency). Also time how long each contraction lasts (duration). Record at least 4-5 in a row to establish a pattern.

faq.question: What is the difference between Braxton Hicks and real contractions?
faq.answer: Braxton Hicks: irregular, don't get stronger, stop with position change or hydration. Real labor: regular pattern, progressively stronger and closer together, don't stop with rest, may radiate to back. When in doubt, call your provider.
